violations.text: Establishment is lacking an allergen statement on the menu and in the facility. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies was not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Chicken was observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Boxes of food/veggies were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

The cutting boards in the prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets for the prep unit lowboy were observed in poor repair. Gasket must be replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Establishment must attain stoppers for 3-bay sink, and properly sanitize equipment and utensils. 3-bay resource was provided to PIC at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2024

Prep unit was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Product had been in unit for less than 2 hours and moved into the walk in cooler at the time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment is to have unit serviced and can not store any TCS foods in unit until it is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, shrimp was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Shrimp was moved to sheet pans at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Knives were observed being stored in between the prep-unit. Knives must be stored on a clean, dry surface. Knives were moved at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen and prep area very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
Prep unit lowboy gaskets were observed in poor repair. Establishment must replace gaskets. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Hood system was observed with an accumulation of grease and dust build up. Hood system is to be cleaned and serviced as needed. Establishment is scheduled to have service done on Monday 8/12/2024. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Scouring pad was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Scouring pad was moved at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Establishment must attain stoppers for 3-bay sink, and properly sanitize equipment and utensils. 3-bay resource was provided to PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2024

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings under 3-bay sink. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrective action - droppings must be cleaned so that new activity can be monitored. Establishment is to increase pest control as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Knives were observed being stored in between the prep-unit. Knives must be stored on a clean, dry surface. Knives were moved at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen and prep area very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips and test the sanitizing solution before sanitizing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the various locations have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
Hood system was observed with an accumulation of grease and dust build up. Hood system is to be cleaned and serviced as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
